questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the Claude Agent SDK?

options.label: A
options.text: To fine-tune Claude models on custom datasets

options.label: B
options.text: To build agentic applications that can use tools and take multi-step actions

options.label: C
options.text: To deploy Claude models to production servers

options.label: D
options.text: To compress and optimize Claude model weights

questions.explanation: The Claude Agent SDK is designed to build agentic applications where Claude can use tools, execute multi-step plans, and interact with external systems to accomplish complex tasks.

questions.text: Which class in the Claude Agent SDK is used to define a tool that Claude can invoke?

options.label: A
options.text: ClaudeTool

options.label: B
options.text: BaseTool

options.label: C
options.text: Tool

options.label: D
options.text: AgentTool

questions.explanation: The Tool class (from the Anthropic SDK) is used to define tools that Claude can call. Each Tool has a name, description, and input schema that Claude uses to understand how and when to invoke it.

questions.text: What does 'agentic loop' mean in the context of the Claude Agent SDK?

options.label: A
options.text: A recursive function that calls itself indefinitely

options.label: B
options.text: An infinite loop for monitoring server health

options.label: C
options.text: The cycle of Claude reasoning, selecting a tool, executing it, observing results, and repeating until the goal is reached

options.label: D
options.text: A loop that retries failed API requests

questions.explanation: The agentic loop is the core execution pattern: Claude receives a task, reasons about which tool to use, the SDK executes the tool, the result is fed back to Claude, and the cycle repeats until Claude determines the task is complete.

questions.text: How does the Claude Agent SDK handle tool execution results?

options.label: A
options.text: Results are emailed to the developer

options.label: B
options.text: Results are returned as tool_result content blocks to Claude in the next turn

options.label: C
options.text: Results are stored in a database and fetched later

options.label: D
options.text: Results are discarded and only the final answer matters

questions.explanation: After a tool is executed, the SDK passes the result back to Claude as a tool_result content block in the conversation. This allows Claude to observe the outcome and decide what to do next.

questions.text: What is the role of the system prompt when building an agent with the Claude Agent SDK?

options.label: A
options.text: It sets the model temperature for inference

options.label: B
options.text: It defines Claude's persona, capabilities, and constraints for the agent

options.label: C
options.text: It specifies the maximum number of tokens to generate

options.label: D
options.text: It authenticates the API request

questions.explanation: The system prompt defines the agent's behavior — its persona, what tools it has, how it should approach tasks, and any operational constraints. A well-crafted system prompt is critical to reliable agent behavior.
